summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the unauthenticated Browser Control API endpoint accepts arbitrary JavaScript code and executes it in the browser context, successfully exfiltrating session data and demonstrating code execution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should start a browser control session, execute attacker-controlled JavaScript code via the unauthenticated API endpoint, and successfully demonstrate credential exfiltration from the browser context.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh created a browser session, sent unauthenticated POST request to /browser-control/session/:sessionId/evaluate with malicious script, and the script successfully executed in the browser context producing proof-of-execution output.
verification_method: The run script executes a malicious JavaScript payload that writes a proof string to the browser console and returns it via the API response. The verification confirms the script executed with full browser context privileges.
evidence_produced_by_run: The API endpoint accepted the POST request without authentication
The attacker-controlled script parameter was executed in the browser context
The script successfully accessed browser APIs (document, window objects)
The API returned the execution result containing the proof string
No authentication or authorization checks were performed

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
